free_text: On-line measurements of rare-gas fission yields
in 14 MeV neutron fission
Tables.
Table of cumulative yields.
Thesis, A.O.6032, Cnrs.
Laboratoire de chimie nucleaire.
SAMES 14 MeV neutron generator.
Electrostatically accelerated deuteron
beam on solid titanium tritide target.
On line isotope separator, ISOL.
Quantitative measurements of rare-gas
activities were performed using a 4pi beta-counting
device detecting fission product activities on a
moving tape located at the end of the isotope
separator.
Two sources were collected from each irradiation,
the first was collected during irradiation and
counted, the second was collected after irradiation
and was counted to allow separation of independent
and cumulative yields.
Independent yields are derived from the measured
ratios of independent yields of Xe and Kr to the
corresponding cumulative yields of I and Br.
4Pi proportional counter for beta detection.
- All decay
parameters
Corrections were made for the behaviour of the rare
gases through the on-line system, for daughter
activity growing at the collector end, and for
counting losses due to absorption of betas.
Total absolute uncertainties are given at
one standard deviation. No details.
SD+NO: Entry revised.

Relative chain yields are derived from the relative
cumulative yields of Kr and Xe, together with
measured or extrapolated fractional cumulative yields
Absolute yields were obtained by normalising with a
factor from the weighted averages of the ratios
between these yields and absolute yields from
Meek (see REL-REF).
Table 4a from Nucl.Phys.,189(1972)556.
SD: BIB updated.
Natural uranium
No correction made for the U-235 content.
Relative chain yields are derived from the relative
cumulative yields of Kr and Xe, together with
measured or extrapolated fractional cumulative yields
Absolute yields were obtained by normalising with a
factor from the weighted averages of the ratios
between these yields and absolute yields from
Meek or Gorman(see REL-REF).
Table 4a from Nucl.Phys.,189(1972)556.
SD: BIB updated.
In the absence of known fractional cumulative yields
the experimental AP values were used to derive ZP
values. Assuming the same variation of ZP with A as
for thermal fission, and a Gaussian distribution
for independent yields, the fractional cumulative
yields were estimated.
Normalisation factors are the weighted averages of
ratios between the relative yields and absolute value
given by H.R. von Gunten, Actinides rev. 1(1969)275
Table 4b from Nucl.Phys.,189(1972)556.
SD: BIB updated.
In the absence of measured fractional cumulative
yields, values are estimated from the experimental
values of AP, from which ZP values are derived.
It is assumed that ZP varies with A as for thermal
fission, and that independent yields follow a
Gaussian distribution in Z.
Normalisation factors are the weighted averages of
ratios between the relative yields and absolute
values from K.D.Borden (see REL-REF).
Table 4b from Nucl.Phys.,189(1972)556.
